ref: Jour: Physical Review, Part C, Nuclear Physics, Vol.64, p.065803 (2001)
title: Proton capture cross section of Sr isotopes and their importance for nucleosynthesis of proton-rich nuclides

x4freetext: Proton capture cross section of Sr isotopes and their
importance for nucleosynthesis of proton-rich nuclides

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Physical Review, Part C, Nuclear Physics, Vol.64, p.065803 (2001)
Jour: Nuclear Physics, Section A, Vol.688, p.90 (2001)

x4freetext: Preliminary.

x4codes.hlp: Institute for Nuclear Research (ATOMKI), Debrecen, Hungary
NCSR Demokritos, Aghia Paraskevi, Athens, Greece
Institute for Nuclear Research (ATOMKI), Debrecen, Hungary

x4freetext: Natural Sr-F/2 targets evaporated onto thick carbon
backings were used. The number of target atoms was
determined by proton induced X-Ray emission. The
results were checked by Rutherford backscattering of
alpha particles.
To detect the backscattered protons and this
way to monitor the target stability.
To detect gamma-rays of produced isotopes.
The irradiations lasted between 6 and 24 hours
with a beam current of typically 5 -10 mA.
The gamma spectra were taken for at least 48
hours.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: No information about source of uncertainty
With calculations "Non-Smoker" and "Most" codes
astrophysical reaction rates derived from new data
S-factor is given on figs 6, 7.
Last checking has been done.
NDS(VZ): REL-REF: ND/B->NDS in 002,003,005,

Subents 009-011 deleted (see ADD-RES). ERR-T->DATA-ERR.

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Nuclear Data Sheets, Vol.62, p.327 (1991)

x4freetext: Decay parameters of
the Y product nuclei
- By authors:
1. At 2.67 MeV bombarding energy the 87-Sr(p,n)
channel opens; hence above this energy only the sum of
the cross section of 86-Sr(p,gamma)87-Y and 87-Sr(p,n)
reactions can be deduced. However, calculations show
that in the investigated energy region the (p,n)
channel is much - about two orders of magnitude -
weaker than the (p,gamma).
2. The resulting cross sections are significantly lower
than the values of the model calculations.

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Nuclear Data Sheets, Vol.62, p.327 (1991)

x4freetext: Decay parameters of
the Y product nuclei
- By authors:
1.  At 2.67 MeV bombarding energy the 87-Sr(p,n)
channel opens; hence above this energy only the sum of
the cross section of 86-Sr(p,gamma)87-Y and 87-Sr(p,n)
reactions can be deduced.  However, calculations show
that in the investigated energy region the (p,n)
channel is much - about two orders of magnitude -weaker
than the (p,gamma).
2. The resulting cross sections are significantly lower
than the values of the model calculations.

compNotes: CONVERTED INC-ENERGY C.M. TO LAB: EN_LAB=EN_CM*1.0117313

x4freetext: - By authors:
1.  At 2.67 MeV bombarding energy the 87-Sr(P,N)
channel opens; hence above this energy only the sum of
the cross section of 86-Sr(P,g)87-Y and 87-Sr(P,N)
reactions can be deduced.  However, calculations show
that in the investigated energy region the (P,N)
channel is much - about two orders of magnitude -weaker
than the (P,g).
2. The resulting cross sections are significantly lower
than the values of the model calculations.
